name: Coder-Next: session transfer
summary: Task-disjoint session history raises coverage and decode throughput; circular data is shown only as a ceiling.

model.name: Qwen3-Coder-Next MXFP4 MoE
notes: Measured on one Windows workstation; recalibrate elsewhere.

runs.label: Generic corpus (legacy protocol)
notes: Retained for context; ProtocolFingerprint intentionally blocks comparison with the V2.2 baseline.

runs.label: Session history
notes: Non-circular: three prior tasks, evaluated on an unseen task.

runs.label: Same-task ceiling (legacy protocol)
notes: Circular ceiling; not a deployable recommendation.

limitations: The protocol-compatible session arm measures +22.2%; the earlier +19.3% headline mixed build fingerprints and is not reproduced here.
Legacy generic and same-task arms remain visible but cannot be compared with the V2.2 baseline.
The same-task arm is circular and must not be selected as the recommended production configuration.

provenance.transformation: Prompt text and local paths removed; only activation counts, expert ids, protocol metadata, and timings retained.
